title: Fuel Price Wahala Eases: Petrol Now Below ₦900/Litre After Dangote Cut
content:
Naija motorists, how far? If you’ve hit the road recently, you probably noticed something refreshing at the pumps—petrol now dey go for less than ₦900 per litre in many parts of the country! This relief came after the Dangote Petroleum Refinery dropped its ex-depot price by ₦40, and marketers have finally started adjusting their rates to match.

In Lagos and Ogun States, petrol now dey sell between ₦875 to ₦890 per litre, compared to the ₦925–₦960 range we dey see just last week. Even MRS Oil, one of Dangote’s major distributors, dropped their Lagos price from ₦925 to ₦885. Na small win, but for Naija wey every kobo counts, e still matter!

The story long sha. You remember say just a few weeks ago, fuel price jump because crude oil price rise reach almost $80 per barrel, thanks to wahala between Israel and Iran. But as peace return for the Middle East, crude prices calm down. Dangote quickly adjust him price to reflect the global change now down to ₦840 per litre from ₦880.

At first, most marketers no gree follow. Dem still dey sell high to clear old stock na business logic. According to Billy Gillis-Harry from the Petroleum Products Retail Outlet Owners Association of Nigeria, make dem sell the old fuel first before bringing in new stock at reduced cost. “Nobody wan lose ₦100 per litre,” the man talk.

But by Sunday, our street gist show say most filling stations don change their price boards. Along the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, stations like AP, SGR, As-Sallam, Akiavic, and SAO now dey sell fuel at ₦875–₦890. Even big names like Rainoil and NIPCO dey adjust small small.

This na sharp u-turn from just three weeks ago, when petrol price touch ₦960 for some places in the north. With crude oil now below $70 per barrel, the downward trend fit continue—if no fresh wahala arise for the global market.

For Naija drivers, this na breather, even if small. E no be total solution, but at least, e no dey go up like NEPA pole again.
excerpt: Following Dangote Refinery’s price slash, many filling stations across Lagos and Ogun have adjusted pump prices below ₦900 per litre, bringing mild relief to Naija motorists.
commentary: The recent petrol price dip shows how international events, like the Israel-Iran ceasefire, dey affect daily life for Naija people. With Dangote’s refinery leading the charge, we fit dey hopeful for more stability—at least when war no dey shake crude oil prices. But make we no sleep; fuel market still dey volatile. This trend fit help small traders, keke riders, and car owners, but long-term relief go need better policy balance and local refining strategy.

title: Peter Obi Declares 2027 Ambition: “I Only Need 4 Years”
content:
Na so we hear am o, Peter Obi don officially throw hat into the 2027 presidential ring. In a series of bold statements, the former Anambra governor and ex-Labour Party presidential candidate said he will contest again, this time under a Labour–ADC coalition.

Obi, who still dey officially under Labour Party, explained say the coalition na to strengthen their chances in 2027. “We have all agreed to work together in coalition,” he talk. The man no dey play!

And guess wetin? Obi say he only needs one term—four years—to show Naija the meaning of good governance. “I said it, I don’t need more than four years,” he declared with confidence.

He also touched on serious national issues wey dey pain everybody:

🔹 Insecurity – “From Day 1, I will tackle it head-on. I will do whatever it takes to secure Nigeria.”
🔹 Northern Nigeria – “If I’m president, the North will celebrate me. The uncultivated lands there are Naija’s biggest asset.”
🔹 Federal character – “Appointments will be based on merit and representation. I won’t be Minister of Petroleum like some others.”
🔹 Corruption – “I left ₦36bn behind as governor. Who does that if not someone with integrity?”
🔹 On Nnamdi Kanu – “Why is he still being held? It doesn’t make sense.”

He didn’t stop there o. Obi throw shade at President Tinubu’s administration, accusing it of lacking compassion and borrowing recklessly. “Tinubu’s government has borrowed more than Yar’Adua, Jonathan, and Buhari combined,” he claim.

As usual, Obi’s talk dey make Nigerians reason. Na man wey dey waka his talk. Remember say as governor, he used a Peugeot 406 while others dey fly private jets. “If your people are dying, the president should be ready to die too,” he said emotionally.

Whether you like am or not, Obi don enter the 2027 gist full-time—and the campaign heat don dey build small small.
excerpt: Peter Obi has declared his intention to run for president in 2027 under a coalition with ADC, promising to deliver results within four years, tackle insecurity, and govern with compassion and accountability.
commentary: Peter Obi’s 2027 ambition is already sparking major conversations across Nigeria’s political space. His message of integrity, accountability, and urgent reform is striking a chord—especially among youths and middle-class voters tired of the status quo. But Naija politics no be beans. The coalition with ADC may open fresh doors or stir internal drama. One thing sure: Obi dey position himself as a serious contender, with or without establishment backing.

title: JAMB Sets 150 Cut-off for 2025 Admissions, Bars Under-16 Students
content:
Naija Students, Una Hear? JAMB Don Raise Cut-Off for 2025!

If you dey plan enter university for 2025, make you sit up o! JAMB and tertiary education big wigs don agree say 150 na the new benchmark wey go determine who fit enter university this year. That one no be all — anybody wey never clock 16? Just calm down, because you no go fit apply, at least for now.

This latest update land during the annual policy meeting for admissions wey happen for Abuja on Tuesday. The new cut-off na 150 for universities, 100 for polytechnics and colleges of education, and 140 for nursing schools. So, if you score below these numbers, just begin look plan B.

Last year, na 140 dem set for universities, so the bar don go higher. JAMB Registrar, Prof. Ishaq Oloyede, and Education Minister, Dr. Tunji Alausa, even try push for 160 — but some VCs no gree. Some propose 130, others shout 140, but finally, everybody jam head for 150.

Prof Oloyede sharply talk say any school wey set their own mark below this new level must upgrade or forget am. "Every institution should maintain its own approved minimum," the man yarn.

Meanwhile, FG don stamp 16 years old as the minimum age for anybody wey wan enter university. Before nko? Dem talk say this age go help balance brain maturity and school readiness. But if you be Einstein pikin or super genius, dem say special consideration go dey but it must be documented well.

Minister Alausa warn schools say any admission outside JAMB's official Central Admissions Processing System (CAPS) na illegal. So no go dey play smart with fake age or secret backdoor entry.

In short: If your JAMB score no reach 150 or you never reach 16, take am easy. Wait small, prepare better. Education still dey your front.
excerpt: JAMB has raised the 2025 university cut-off mark to 150 and made 16 years the minimum admission age, warning schools to comply or face consequences.
commentary: This new JAMB policy dey important for both students and parents. As JAMB dey try boost academic standards, raising cut-off marks and age limits go help filter mature, serious candidates. But the wahala be say many students still dey underage by SS3, especially with fast school systems. E go good make schools align their curricula with this new age policy, so students no go dey stuck. For now, everyone must just plan well and follow the right steps to avoid disappointment.

title: Buhari’s Final Journey: From London to Daura, Nigeria Bids Farewell
content:
Naija stood still as the body of former President Muhammadu Buhari began its final journey on Tuesday, July 15, 2025. From London to Katsina, and finally to Daura, the man many knew as “Mai Gaskiya” the Honest One was laid to rest in the same hometown where his life began.

The military aircraft carrying Buhari’s remains touched down at Umaru Musa Yar’Adua International Airport around 2:00 p.m. The atmosphere was heavy. Soldiers formed a guard of honour. Close friends, family members, and government officials stood solemn as the draped casket emerged.

Vice President Kashim Shettima led the federal delegation that accompanied the body from the UK. President Bola Tinubu, who had arrived earlier, received the casket alongside Governor Dikko Radda and First Lady Senator Oluremi Tinubu. The presence of these figures highlighted just how deeply intertwined Buhari’s story was with Nigeria’s political evolution.

Once the formalities were done, the late president’s body was quietly placed in a hearse. No unnecessary fanfare just a slow, respectful road trip to Daura, a nod to Buhari’s lifelong preference for humility and staying close to his roots.

The convoy’s journey was met with waves of mourners. From Katsina to Daura, the roads were lined with people: some weeping, others whispering prayers, many simply watching in silence. It was a moment of collective remembrance.

In Daura, preparations had been made for the Islamic burial rites, which were performed in line with his faith. For a man who ruled Nigeria twice—first as a military leader in the 1980s, and later as a democratically elected president—this quiet, dignified burial captured both his contradictions and convictions.

Whether you saw him as a disciplinarian, a patriot, or a polarizing figure, Buhari’s place in Nigeria’s story is undeniable. On this day, even critics paused to honour his service and legacy.

From military barracks to Aso Rock and now back to the soil of Daura, Buhari’s final homecoming was deeply Nigerian simple, spiritual, and soaked in the naija vibe of respect for the dead.

Wetin go help you grow faster and get more money, TikTok or YouTube Shorts?

Make we break am down AmeboGist style 👇

💥 TikTok: The Fast Fame Machine

Pros:

  • Fast virality: One trend fit give you 100k views overnight.

  • Naija algorithm dey active – Nigerians dey blow steady.

  • Music selection and filters sweet die.

  • Dey perfect for funny skits, dances, voiceovers, and POV-style videos.

Cons:

  • Monetization still be wahala for Nigeria.

  • Engagement fit drop without warning.

  • You go need post consistently (like 4–5 times per week).

🔴 YouTube Shorts: The Long Term Pay Machine

Pros:

  • YouTube Partner Program dey open for Shorts — you fit earn real money.

  • Better for vlog style content, tutorials, lifestyle gist.

  • Viewers dey more serious, e good if you dey build personal brand.

Cons:

  • Growth dy slowe pass TikTok.

  • You need sabi thumbnail + title game.

  • Some trends dey come late to Shorts.
